name: turtle
description: Support for RDF/Turtle and SAMM Aspect Models

commands.title: Restart and reconnect to Language Server

configuration.title: Semantic Models
configuration.description: Configuration settings for Semantic Models extension.

turtle.languageServerSettings.activateEm.description: When enabled, the extension starts the SAMM CLI language server process at the path configured below. When disabled, an external language server must be started manually.

turtle.languageServerSettings.automaticU.description: Automatically check for updates of the SAMM CLI language server and notify when a new version is available.

turtle.languageServerSettings.sammCliPat.description: Path to the SAMM CLI executable or jar file to use as the language server. Can be downloaded / set via the 'Select SAMM CLI Executable' command.

turtle.languageServerSettings.serverPort.description: TCP port used to connect to the Turtle/SAMM language server.

turtle.languageServerSettings.traceLevel.description: Controls the verbosity of language client protocol tracing in the output channel.

walkthroughs.title: Getting Started with Semantic Models Extension
walkthroughs.description:
Learn how to use the Semantic Models extension to validate your RDF/Turtle files and SAMM Aspect Models.
This walkthrough will guide you through the key features of the extension, including validating your RDF/Turtle files and SAMM Aspect Models using the integrated language server.

steps.title: Selecting a SAMM CLI Executable
steps.description:
Initial setup requires you to either download or manually select a SAMM CLI, containing the required SAMM Language Server 
[Download or Select SAMM CLI](command:turtle.selectSammCliExecutable)

media.altText: Example of selecting a SAMM CLI executable

steps.title: Validating RDF/Turtle Files and SAMM Aspect Models
steps.description: To validate an RDF/Turtle file or a SAMM Aspect Model, simply open it in VS Code. The extension will automatically connect to the language server and display any validation issues in the Problems panel. You can also trigger validation manually using the 'Validate document now' command from the Command Palette.

media.altText: Example of validation issues shown in the Problems panel

scripts.pretest: npm run build && npm run lint
